name: Founding Charter: Meridian Reach
description: Carry ten thousand embryos, a seed vault, and a legal charter four and a half centuries to Meridian Reach, and leave a living settlement behind.

completion_reward.log: Feeding a colony into being taught the ship's gardeners lessons a mere voyage never could, and the agriculture decks are the wiser for it.

name: Deep Vein Survey: Karst Belt
description: Three and a half centuries threading the Karst Belt, charting extractable veins and shipping proof-of-yield cores back by relay.

completion_reward.log: Three centuries of threading veins and reading rock left the survey crews sharper than they began — a working knowledge of extraction the engineering bay keeps.

name: Charter of Discovery: The Veiled Expanse
description: Nearly four centuries into the unmapped dark past the last relay, charting a lightless expanse the founders only ever named. Bring back the map, and the naming rights.

name: Relief Mandate: The Tarssen Drift
description: A generation ship went silent in the Tarssen Drift, three centuries down the dark. Someone may still be breathing when you reach them. Take aboard who remains, and answer for every berth you cannot fill.

name: Coronal Tap: The Ember Star
description: Three centuries skimming a red dwarf's corona for raw energy, feeding a collector array the size of a city block.

name: Seedfall Mandate: Ashgrove
description: A four-century drop of a second settlement — a hedge against the first. Half the charter, twice the doubt.

name: Beacon Line: The Starfall Verge
description: Four centuries laying a chain of relay beacons out to the Starfall Verge, so the ships that come after need not cross the dark blind.

name: Recovery Writ: The Hollow Fleet
description: A refugee convoy broke apart in transit generations ago, its lifeboats still drifting on dead reckoning three centuries down the dark. Find what holds air, and choose which to reach first.

completion_reward.log: The hollow fleet gave up more than its living: among the dead escorts drifting the wreck-line was a warship's spinal mount, its firing chain intact, salvageable by a crew willing to cut it free. It came home in the hold — a heavy, ugly reminder that the dark is not always empty of teeth, waiting on a drydock to bolt it to the spine.

name: Warden Mandate: The Cinder Lane
description: Near four centuries standing guard over a lawless shipping lane — escorting convoys, answering distress calls, and pulling the wrecked from the dark before the scavengers do.

milestones.name: On station at the Cinder Lane

name: The Long Dark: Charter to the Rim
description: Six centuries to the galaxy's ragged edge and back, charting the black between the last stars. The founders dreamed of this run and never dared it. Only a storied ship should.

name: Deep Camp: The Cinder Vein
description: Sail ninety years to a metal-rich cinder world and then stay — three centuries camped in orbit, working the vein dry, while whole generations are born and die having known only the worksite and the dark.

milestones.name: Vein worked to the marginal seams

name: Residency: The Hearthfall Accord
description: Carry an embassy to a struggling inhabited world and live among them for eight generations — not a landing and a departure but a long residency, tending a fragile people until an accord can hold, then coming home.

name: Double Survey: The Paired Deeps
description: A single voyage to two uncharted anomalies on the same long arc — survey the first, then burn on to the second before turning for home. Two destinations, one crew, and a return that waits behind both.

milestones.name: Both deeps logged, course for home

name: Salvage Writ: The Long Tow
description: A titan-hulled colony ship died on its outbound burn three centuries ago, its reactors cold, its holds and archives intact. The reach out is short; the writ is to grapple the derelict and drag it home. The tow is the voyage — a quarter-millennium hauling a dead ship heavier than your own across the deep dark, its mass on your line the whole way.

completion_reward.log: Grappling the dead titan, the salvage crews found more than archives in its silent engineering spaces — an experimental drive coil, generations ahead of anything this ship carried, cold but whole. It rode home in the hold, a prize the writ never thought to promise, waiting for a drydock bold enough to fit it.

milestones.name: Founder archives lifted from the dead ship
Tow rig certified for the long haul

name: Survey Writ: The Far Crossing
description: The target is a structure at the very edge of the reachable dark — so far that the crossing to it is the whole of the trial. Three centuries of one-way burn into unmapped space, with no port, no relief, and no turning aside; a brief survey when the ship finally arrives; and then home along a course that, for the first time, is charted. The hard part is getting there, and no one who casts off will live to see it done.

milestones.name: The structure resolves — arrival at last
Survey complete, course home laid in

name: Survey Writ: The Sunward Dive
description: Not to mine the star, but to read it — the writ is to thread a research hull through the outer atmosphere of a dying sun and bring home what no instrument has recorded from the safe dark: the death-throes of a star, sampled from inside its reach. The operation is a long, slow dive under flare and radiation, the whole of it spent where no ship is meant to be.

milestones.name: First skim of the outer atmosphere
The dying core read at last
Survey closed, course set for the dark

completion_reward.log: The ship dived into a dying star and came out with a hold of data no archive in the reachable dark could match — the education and culture stores enriched beyond price by what the crossing saw.

name: Relief Writ: The Sunset Coast
description: A colony under a sun the astronomers have already buried: its dimming is computed, its end dated, and the writ is not to save the world — no one can — but to sail four centuries to its failing coast, carry off what a single hull can carry, and stand with a people through the hours of their ending. A mission with its tragedy written into it before the ship was laid, and its hardest choices set on a clock the dead read from the stars.

milestones.name: First lift off the failing coast
The last shore cleared that could be
Holds full, course set for home

name: The Seedbearers' Writ
description: A seedworld consortium will entrust the greening of a barren candidate world to one hull only: the ship that carries the Verdant Kin, whose gardeners alone are trusted to coax a living biosphere out of dead rock across the generations it takes.

completion_reward.log: The Verdant Kin coaxed a living world out of dead rock, and came away having learned things about growing that no ship's garden ever teaches — a depth of green craft the agriculture decks will carry forever.

name: Karst Belt Works: Permanent Extraction
description: The Karst Belt is charted and its richest veins proven; now a consortium wants them worked in earnest. Return to the belt and raise a permanent extraction settlement — refineries, habs, a self-sustaining crew — the mission that only exists because an earlier voyage did the surveying, and only for a hull whose word is still good.

completion_reward.log: The great works at the Karst Belt were raised, run, and certified, and the crews that built them came home masters of a craft the founders had only sketched — large-scale extraction learned in the doing, a competence now written into the engineering bay for good.

name: Sanctuary Run: The Talleth Exodus
description: A failing habitat-world needs its people carried to safety across four centuries of dark, and the consortium arranging it will not hand that trust to just any hull. They want a ship with a name — a reputation for treating the vulnerable as kin, for the sort of mercy that does not calculate — because a people entrusting its children to a passing stranger deserves a stranger already known to be kind.

completion_reward.log: Four centuries of carrying the frail and the frightened made the ship's medics the finest kind — practiced in keeping the vulnerable alive across a crossing, a depth of care the medical bay keeps.

abandonment.log: The ship turned for home with its holds far from full and a shore of the frail and the frightened left behind it, and the dark — which had trusted this hull to be the one that came — marked that this time it had not. A relief run given up hardens something in a crew, a kindness they could not in the end keep, and a name for it travels: that here is a ship that will leave you, if the leaving is easier.

name: Enforcement Writ: The Vane Default
description: A colony consortium has defaulted, and its distant backers want their assets recovered from a settlement that will not give them up willingly — a cold, unlovely job of stripping a place that people still call home. No hull with a soft reputation would take it, and the backers would not trust a soft hull to finish it; they want a ship known for doing the hard thing without flinching, and they pay accordingly.

completion_reward.log: Four centuries of stripping a place people still called home left their mark on the ship — a crew grown practiced at the cold thing done without flinching, and a name in the dark grown colder to match.

abandonment.log: In the end the ship could not finish it — could not go on stripping a place that people still called home — and turned for its own port with the writ undone and the backers unpaid. It cost the ship its fee and its name for hard reliability both; but a crew that discovers, at the last, a line it will not cross has learned something about itself that a colder ship never will, even as the dark marks it down as a hull that folds.

name: The Ark Charter: Ten Thousand Sleepers
description: Ten thousand colonists sleep frozen in the holds — a whole world's founding population, entrusted to this ship to carry across three centuries of dark to a waiting shore. They cannot be replaced and they cannot be woken early. The cold banks will fail here and there no matter how well they are tended, and the dark will take its share; the charter does not ask that none be lost, only that as many as possible arrive alive. Everything the ship does for three hundred years is, in the end, in service of a number that can only ever go down.

completion_reward.log: The waiting shore woke its sleepers one by one, and most of them opened their eyes on a new world. The ship that carried them there is remembered, in that colony's first histories, as the vessel that kept faith with the frozen — and the crew carried home the rare knowledge that they had delivered a world its people, very nearly whole.
